acceptance_criteria: No persisted artifact or served dashboard response contains raw API keys, common token patterns, private key blocks, configured secret values, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw TASK.md bodies, raw document bodies, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.

All docs, run artifact, manifest, log, planner, Codex event, diff, eval, validation, TASK.md dashboard summary, latest-run dashboard summary, next-action dashboard summary, project document shortcut data, and dashboard-visible writes are redacted before persistence or display.
Traversal attempts using relative, absolute, encoded, or symlinked paths cannot read or write outside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots.
jj run accepts only plan files that resolve inside the configured workspace after documented path resolution, canonicalization, and symlink evaluation.

External, traversal, absolute-escape, encoded-escape, symlink-escape, unreadable, malformed, and secret-looking plan inputs are rejected before content read or artifact persistence with deterministic sanitized diagnostics.
In-workspace relative, process-cwd-resolved, and absolute in-workspace plan paths continue to work in dry-run and non-dry-run modes.
Plan input and Codex artifact symlink swaps are rejected without reading, persisting, or rendering out-of-root content.
Low-information configured sensitive literals such as true, false, null, and none do not become global redaction values.
jj serve displays intended docs and run artifacts through guarded paths only, including dashboard, detail, history, comparison, and audit export routes.
Duplicate hostile query values and secret-looking or token-like run IDs are denied deterministically without reflecting rejected values.
Command records include only sanitized argv, status, timing, cwd label, and result metadata and do not persist raw command text or raw environment dumps.
Persisted manifests and general JSON artifacts use safe path labels and do not store raw workspace, plan, external config, git, repository, validation command, or baseline paths.

Git baseline, git diff, and validation stdout and stderr artifacts are sanitized before persistence and expose redaction evidence only as booleans, counts, sanitized categories, and safe relative labels.

Manifest and dashboard security diagnostics expose only counts, booleans, root labels, relative-safe roots, sanitization status, dry-run parity status, sanitized failure categories, sanitized validation output redaction evidence, and sanitized git diff redaction evidence.

Dry-run and non-dry-run produce equivalent redaction, boundary, diagnostic, validation output, planner handoff metadata, git diff redaction behavior, artifact persistence, and safe path label persistence.

Planner request, Codex fallback prompt, agent handoff JSON, planning context snapshot, task proposal reason, event, manifest, and log fields use guarded sanitizer helpers and do not persist unsafe raw payloads.

Missing, malformed, partial, older, denied, stale, tampered, or internally inconsistent run metadata renders deterministic denied, unknown, unavailable, or sanitized states on detail, history, compare, audit export, dashboard latest-run, and dashboard next-action surfaces without server errors or unsafe fallback fields.

Run detail, run history, run comparison, audit export, dashboard latest-run, and dashboard next-action handlers obtain run metadata through shared sanitized DTO or helper paths and do not perform handler-local raw manifest, artifact, command, environment, diff, validation output, or filesystem reads after route input parsing.
Dashboard root shows TASK.md summary counts for done, in-progress, pending, blocked, and total when a valid in-workspace docs/TASK.md exists.
Dashboard root shows the first actionable pending or in-progress task with sanitized ID, category, status, and title.
When all TASK.md tasks are done, the dashboard shows no runnable tasks without opening a new task or implying failure.
Missing, unreadable, malformed, or denied TASK.md renders deterministic unavailable or unknown states without server errors and without reflecting unsafe paths or raw parse payloads.

No raw TASK.md body, unsafe absolute path, secret-looking value, omission placeholder, raw artifact body, manifest body, command text, or environment value is persisted or rendered by the dashboard task summary.

Dashboard root shows a compact latest-run summary when at least one valid guarded run exists, including only sanitized run ID label, status, provider or result label, evaluation state, timestamp label, and guarded links to existing run views.
Dashboard root renders deterministic none, unavailable, or unknown latest-run states when no runs exist or latest metadata is missing, denied, malformed, partial, stale, or internally inconsistent.
Latest-run selection is deterministic when timestamps are missing, malformed, duplicated, or tied.

No dashboard latest-run response contains raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, raw command text, raw environment values, validation payloads, diff bodies, unsafe absolute paths, denied payloads, token-like values, configured secrets, private key blocks, or omission placeholders.

Dashboard root renders a compact next-action summary for pending, in-progress, all-done, missing TASK.md, unavailable TASK.md, failed latest-run, needs_work latest-run, unknown latest-run, and no-run states.

Dashboard next-action precedence is deterministic: continue the first in-progress task, then start the first pending task, then review the latest run when no task is runnable and the latest-run state needs review, otherwise render all-done, unavailable, unknown, or none.

Dashboard next-action data is derived only from shared sanitized TASK.md summary and latest-run DTO or helper output, with no handler-local raw manifest, artifact, command, environment, diff, validation output, or filesystem reads after route input parsing.

Dashboard next-action action labels and guarded links are deterministic, sanitized, and never reflect denied paths, token-like values, configured secrets, private key blocks, omission placeholders, raw TASK.md bodies, raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, validation payloads, or diff bodies.
Existing TASK.md summary and latest-run summary dashboard behavior remains unchanged when the next-action summary is rendered.
Focused Go serve/dashboard tests cover task-driven, run-driven, all-done, missing, malformed or unknown, denied, unavailable, hostile label, and no-run next-action cases.

No new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, scanners, artifact uploads, broad file browsing, or unrelated user-facing features are recommended as part of healthy release-gate follow-up work, dashboard latest-run summary work, dashboard next-action summary work, or dashboard project document shortcut work.
Existing TASK.md dashboard summary behavior remains unchanged for valid, all-done, missing, malformed, unreadable, and denied states.

Focused Go regression tests cover redaction, path guards, artifact writing, manifest scrubbing, command metadata, dry-run and non-dry-run parity, diagnostics, serve access, run inspection access, Codex artifact symlink rejection, low-information literal filtering, secret-looking run ID denial, guarded plan input handling, sanitized validation output handling, git diff redaction evidence, planner and Codex handoff sanitization, duplicate hostile query denial, needs_work terminal status, TASK.md parsing, all-done task state, missing or malformed TASK.md state, dashboard latest-run states, dashboard next-action states, dashboard project document shortcut states, sanitizer behavior, and dashboard DTO/render output.

Auto or balanced mode resolves to bugfix only for explicit positive current evidence and does not resolve to bugfix for healthy or negated evidence such as passed validation, passed tests, failed_count zero, no blocker, all tasks done, or no runnable tasks.

Auto or balanced mode resolves to security only for concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence and does not resolve to security from healthy release-gate evidence, completed security guardrail text, plan.md background vision, or absent evidence.
No new runnable task is opened from healthy or absent evidence.

Security remains the selected category for any future concrete regression involving secrets, paths, artifacts, commands, manifests, validation output, planner handoff, TASK.md dashboard summary, dashboard latest-run summary, dashboard next-action summary, project document shortcuts, or dashboard exposure.
Any future security task cites concrete failing evidence from scripts/validate.sh, focused tests, full tests, vet, build, diff-check, CI, confirmed disclosure, or boundary evidence.
Future regression work is scoped only to the confirmed failing behavior and includes focused recurrence coverage.
scripts/validate.sh is documented as the single local and CI release validation entry point and runs the focused security boundary suites, full tests, vet, CLI build, and diff-check.
The release validation entry point and CI wrapper suppress child output and print only deterministic release-gate metadata with fixed labels, categories, booleans, counts, and exit codes.

CI invokes scripts/validate.sh for security-relevant pull request or push changes, fails on non-zero exit, and does not enable continue-on-error, verbose shell tracing, child output passthrough, raw artifact upload, raw manifest upload, raw diff upload, secret echoing, or environment dumps.

Focused regression coverage proves the release validation workflow has exactly one scripts/validate.sh run step, preserves security-relevant triggers, and denies continue-on-error, verbose shell tracing, artifact upload, environment dump, raw manifest, raw diff, and secret-reference constructs.
No completed task is reopened unless scripts/validate.sh, focused Go tests, full tests, vet, build, diff-check, or CI inspection finds a concrete security regression.

A narrow CI release-gate privacy verification may inspect CI, documentation, release-gate behavior, and focused regression coverage, but must patch only concrete gaps and must not add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, scanners, artifact upload behavior, or unrelated user-facing features.
TASK-0039 validation passes through the sanitized release validation entry point.

TASK-0040 verification runs scripts/validate.sh as the only release-gate command, passes with sanitized release-gate metadata, applies no implementation patch, and leaves completed security guardrails closed.
TASK-0041 task proposal guardrail validation passes focused internal run tests and scripts/validate.sh with sanitized release-gate metadata, and completed security guardrails remain closed.
TASK-0042 dashboard TASK.md summary validation passes focused serve tests, full tests, and scripts/validate.sh with sanitized release-gate metadata.
TASK-0043 dashboard latest-run summary validation passes focused serve tests and scripts/validate.sh with sanitized release-gate metadata.
TASK-0044 dashboard next-action summary validation passes focused serve/dashboard tests, full internal serve tests, and scripts/validate.sh with sanitized release-gate metadata.
Dashboard root renders a compact Project Docs section for the fixed allow-list of core documents plan.md, docs/SPEC.md, docs/TASK.md, docs/EVAL.md, and README.md.

Each Project Docs item exposes only a sanitized label, deterministic state, and a guarded link when the document resolves inside the workspace and is readable through guarded document route resolution.
Missing, unreadable, denied, symlink-escape, stale, unknown, and hostile-label Project Docs cases render deterministic safe states without server errors or unsafe payload reflection.

No dashboard Project Docs response contains raw document bodies, unsafe absolute paths, secrets, token-like values, omission placeholders, raw manifests, raw artifacts, validation output, diff bodies, or attacker-controlled denied payloads.
Existing TASK.md summary, latest-run summary, and next-action dashboard behavior remains unchanged when Project Docs shortcuts are rendered.

Focused Go serve/dashboard tests cover present docs, missing docs, unreadable or denied docs, symlink escape, hostile labels, unknown or stale states, docs/EVAL.md route support, and unchanged existing dashboard summaries.
TASK-0045 dashboard project document shortcut validation passes focused serve/dashboard tests, full internal serve tests, and scripts/validate.sh with sanitized release-gate metadata.
Completed security guardrails remain closed after TASK-0045 because validation passed and no current release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence shows a concrete regression.
goals: Generate concrete specs and tasks from a single plan file while preserving an auditable run history.

Keep generated artifacts, served files, plan inputs, workspace mutations, validation outputs, git diff artifacts, planner and Codex handoff metadata, dashboard task summaries, dashboard latest-run summaries, dashboard next-action summaries, dashboard project document shortcuts, and run inspection surfaces inside the resolved workspace or guarded run artifact roots.

Prevent secrets, token-like values, private key material, omission placeholders, raw commands, raw environment values, unsafe paths, raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw TASK.md bodies, raw document bodies, and attacker-controlled denied payloads from being persisted or rendered.

Provide a dashboard-first local jj serve experience with guarded run detail, history, comparison, sanitized audit export views, a compact sanitized current task summary, a compact sanitized latest-run summary, a compact sanitized next-action summary, and guarded shortcuts to fixed core project documents.
Preserve dry-run and non-dry-run workflows with identical redaction, path-boundary, diagnostics, artifact persistence, diff redaction, validation, and planner handoff metadata behavior.
Ensure auto and balanced task proposal resolution selects bugfix only from positive current blocker evidence and does not loop on healthy, negated, or completed-task context.

Ensure auto and balanced task proposal resolution selects security only from concrete current release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence and not from completed guardrail policy or background vision alone.
Enforce plan input path handling so explicitly provided plans cannot escape the configured workspace or leak external sensitive content.
Persist git diff artifacts only after redaction and expose any diff redaction evidence through sanitized metadata only.
Treat stale, partial, malformed, manually edited, or internally inconsistent run metadata as untrusted unless fields are sanitized through guarded DTO helpers.
Use scripts/validate.sh as the single documented release validation entry point for local and CI security release gates.
Keep completed security guardrails closed unless a concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary regression is observed.

Expose only guarded dashboard shortcuts for fixed core project documents so users can reach plan.md, docs/SPEC.md, docs/TASK.md, docs/EVAL.md, and README.md without enabling broad file browsing or raw body exposure on the dashboard root.
non_goals: Provide a multi-user dashboard or authentication system.
Replace git review or guarantee AI output correctness.
Add unrelated user-facing features while unresolved or residual security risks are being verified.

Expose raw commands, environment dumps, secret values, sensitive absolute paths, private key material, token-like strings, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw prompt exports, raw TASK.md bodies, raw document bodies, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Add raw manifest export, raw artifact export, raw diff export, raw prompt export, artifact content bundling, raw artifact upload, raw manifest upload, or raw diff upload.

Add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, broad file import behavior, scanners, redaction algorithm expansion, or unrelated user-facing behavior as part of security validation, CI release-gate follow-up work, dashboard latest-run summary work, dashboard next-action summary work, or dashboard project document shortcut work.
Allow explicitly provided plan files outside the workspace by default.
Reopen completed tasks without fresh failing validation, tests, CI, confirmed disclosure, or boundary regression evidence.
Expose child command stdout or stderr from the release validation entry point or CI wrapper.
requirements: jj run must read a non-empty Markdown plan from the explicitly provided path using documented path resolution behavior, then require the resolved plan file to remain inside the configured workspace boundary.
The resolved cwd value, or process cwd when cwd is absent, is the workspace boundary for generated docs, run artifacts, workspace mutations, and accepted plan input files.

Plan path validation must canonicalize requested paths, reject unsupported encoded traversal and escape forms, evaluate symlinks where applicable, reject traversal attempts, and reject symlink or absolute-path escapes outside the workspace before reading content.
Plan input files must be revalidated through the same guarded path result immediately before readback so symlink replacement cannot cause out-of-workspace reads or persistence.

Rejected external, traversal, absolute-escape, encoded-escape, symlink-escape, unreadable, malformed, or secret-looking plan inputs must produce deterministic sanitized errors without reflecting raw paths, raw OS errors, secrets, token-like values, omission placeholders, or attacker-controlled payloads.
In-workspace relative plans, process-cwd-resolved plans, and absolute in-workspace plans must continue to work in dry-run and non-dry-run modes after canonicalization and symlink evaluation.

Accepted plan content, planner output, Codex output, Codex summaries, command summaries, evaluation output, validation output, docs writes, manifest serialization, event writes, run artifact writes, git diff artifacts, TASK.md dashboard summary data, latest-run dashboard data, next-action dashboard data, project document shortcut data, and dashboard render data must pass through shared redaction before persistence or display.

Manifest and general JSON persistence must use safe workspace, run, relative, or generic path labels and must not store raw workspace, plan, external config, git, repository, validation command, or baseline paths.
Command execution records must persist sanitized argv, cwd labels, status, duration, and result metadata only; raw command text and raw environment values must not be stored or rendered.

Planner requests, Codex fallback prompts, agent handoff JSON, planning context snapshots, task proposal reasons, events, manifests, logs, and planner/fallback metadata must be sanitized before persistence, logging, rendering, or manifest inclusion.

Prompt and context builders must not directly concatenate raw manifests, command output, validation payloads, diff bodies, environment values, denied path inputs, or unsafe artifact bodies into persisted handoff metadata.

Validation stdout and stderr artifacts and git diff artifacts must be sanitized before persistence; evidence reports may include only booleans, counts, sanitized category labels, and safe relative artifact labels.

Dry-run and non-dry-run modes must apply identical plan path validation, redaction, path-boundary, artifact persistence, git diff capture, diff redaction, manifest metadata, security diagnostics, validation output sanitization, planner handoff metadata sanitization, and denial behavior.

jj serve must only serve files from guarded workspace and run artifact roots and must reject traversal, absolute, encoded, secret-looking, token-like, duplicate hostile query, or symlink escape inputs with deterministic denied or unavailable states.
Run IDs may contain only safe letters, numbers, dots, underscores, and dashes, must not use traversal-like forms, and must be rejected when they match configured secrets or common token patterns.
Web run completion handling must treat needs_work as a terminal status.
Codex event and summary outputs must resolve under the guarded run artifact root before launch and must be revalidated through the artifact store before fallback creation, redaction, or readback.
Configured sensitive literals used for redaction must ignore low-information values such as true, false, null, and none.

Run detail, run history, run comparison, run audit export, dashboard latest-run, dashboard next-action, and dashboard project document shortcut handlers must obtain metadata through shared sanitized DTO construction or clearly factored shared helpers, not handler-local raw manifest, artifact, command, environment, diff, validation output, document body, or filesystem reads after route input parsing.

Untrusted, stale, partial, malformed, manually edited, or internally inconsistent manifest fields, artifact references, validation summaries, command metadata, diff metadata, provider fields, timestamps, and next-action hints must render only deterministic denied, unknown, unavailable, or sanitized states.
The jj serve dashboard root must read workspace docs/TASK.md only through existing guarded workspace path helpers and must render task information only from sanitized DTO/helper output.
The dashboard TASK.md summary must parse common existing task formats conservatively and expose only sanitized task IDs, categories, statuses, titles, counts, and deterministic state labels.
The dashboard TASK.md summary must select the first actionable pending or in-progress task as the next task and render only sanitized ID, category, status, and title.

Missing, unreadable, malformed, denied, stale, or all-done TASK.md states must render deterministic unavailable, unknown, or none states without server errors and without reflecting unsafe paths or raw parse payloads.

The jj serve dashboard root must show a compact latest-run summary when at least one valid guarded run exists, using only sanitized run ID label, status, provider or result label, evaluation state, timestamp label, and guarded links to existing run views.

Dashboard latest-run selection must be deterministic when timestamps are missing, malformed, duplicated, or tied, and missing, denied, malformed, partial, stale, or internally inconsistent run metadata must render deterministic none, unavailable, or unknown states without server errors.
Dashboard latest-run rendering must not add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, scanners, artifact upload behavior, or unrelated feature surfaces.
The jj serve dashboard root must show a compact next-action summary derived only from existing sanitized TASK.md summary and latest-run DTO or helper output.

Next-action precedence must be deterministic: continue an in-progress task, then start the first pending task, then review the latest run when no task is runnable and the latest-run state is failed, needs_work, denied, unknown, stale, malformed, unavailable, or internally inconsistent, otherwise render all-done, unavailable, unknown, or none states as applicable.

Next-action labels and guarded links must be fixed, sanitized, and deterministic, and must not reflect denied paths, token-like values, configured secrets, private key blocks, omission placeholders, raw TASK.md bodies, raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, validation payloads, or diff bodies.
Dashboard next-action rendering must not add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, scanners, artifact upload behavior, or unrelated feature surfaces.
The jj serve dashboard root must show a compact Project Docs section for the fixed allow-list of core workspace documents plan.md, docs/SPEC.md, docs/TASK.md, docs/EVAL.md, and README.md.

Project document shortcuts must use shared guarded workspace path helpers and guarded document route resolution; links may be rendered only when the document resolves inside the workspace and is readable through those routes.

Project document shortcut items must expose only sanitized document labels, deterministic state labels, and guarded links; they must not expose raw document bodies, unsafe absolute paths, denied path payloads, secrets, token-like values, omission placeholders, manifests, artifacts, validation output, or diff bodies.

Missing, unreadable, denied, symlink-escape, stale, or unknown project document shortcut states must render deterministic missing, unavailable, denied, or unknown states without server errors and without reflecting unsafe paths or raw payloads.

Project document shortcut rendering must preserve existing dashboard TASK.md summary, latest-run summary, and next-action behavior and must not add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, scanners, artifact upload behavior, broad file browsing, or unrelated feature surfaces.

Auto or balanced task proposal resolution must choose bugfix only from positive current evidence of failed validation, failed tests, failed or blocked runnable tasks, provider failure, panic, fatal error, or regression; healthy or negated evidence must not trigger bugfix resolution.

Auto or balanced task proposal resolution must choose security only from concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence showing a security or privacy regression; healthy release-gate context, completed security guardrail text, plan.md background vision, and absent evidence must not open or reopen security work.
Recent security evidence used for task proposal selection must be reduced to sanitized category labels before influencing future task selection.
Structured SPEC guardrail text must not resolve to hardening, quality, or documentation debt modes by keyword alone when no current actionable evidence exists.
Future security regression tasks must cite exact failing evidence and remain narrowly scoped to the confirmed behavior, with regression coverage for recurrence.

scripts/validate.sh must remain the single documented release validation entry point, run the focused security boundary suites, full tests, vet, CLI build, and diff-check, suppress child command output, emit only deterministic sanitized metadata, and exit non-zero on any failing step.

CI must invoke scripts/validate.sh for security-relevant pull request or push changes, fail on non-zero exit, and must not enable verbose shell tracing, child output passthrough, raw artifact upload, raw manifest upload, raw diff upload, secret echoing, environment dumps, or continue-on-error for the release gate.

Focused regression coverage must verify the release validation workflow invokes exactly one scripts/validate.sh run step for security-relevant triggers and rejects privacy-sensitive constructs including continue-on-error, shell tracing, artifact uploads, environment dumps, raw manifest or diff references, and secret references.
Existing release validation documentation must state that scripts/validate.sh is both the local and CI release gate.

Completed security guardrails must remain closed unless scripts/validate.sh, focused Go security suites, full tests, vet, build, diff-check, or the CI release gate produces a concrete disclosure, path-boundary, artifact, dashboard, planner handoff, validation output, git diff, command metadata, TASK.md dashboard summary, dashboard latest-run summary, dashboard next-action summary, or release-gate output regression.
summary: jj is a CLI that turns a planning file into implementation-ready specs, tasks, Codex runs, validation artifacts, and a local dashboard. Security remains the controlling constraint: generated artifacts, served run-inspection surfaces, explicit plan inputs, command metadata, manifests, validation outputs, git diff artifacts, planner and Codex handoff metadata, run metadata, dashboard task summaries, dashboard latest-run summaries, dashboard next-action summaries, dashboard project document shortcuts, and raw document handling must be guarded, redacted, and kept inside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots. jj serve includes guarded dashboard, run detail, history, comparison, sanitized audit export flows, a compact sanitized TASK.md work-queue summary, a compact sanitized latest-run and evaluation summary, a compact sanitized next-action summary, and compact guarded Project Docs shortcuts on the dashboard root. TASK-0036 through TASK-0041 established and verified the sanitized release gate and tightened task proposal resolution so completed security guardrails remain closed unless concrete validation, test, CI, disclosure, or boundary evidence proves a regression. TASK-0042 added guarded docs/TASK.md parsing and sanitized dashboard DTO/render output for task counts, first actionable work, all-done state, and unavailable or unknown states. TASK-0043 added guarded latest-run dashboard summary selection and rendering with focused serve tests and scripts/validate.sh passing with sanitized release-gate metadata. TASK-0044 added guarded next-action dashboard summary selection and rendering, derived from shared sanitized TASK.md and latest-run helpers, with focused serve tests, full internal serve tests, and scripts/validate.sh passing with sanitized release-gate metadata. TASK-0045 added guarded Project Docs shortcuts for plan.md, docs/SPEC.md, docs/TASK.md, docs/EVAL.md, and README.md using shared guarded route resolution, with focused serve/dashboard tests, full internal serve tests, and scripts/validate.sh passing with sanitized release-gate metadata.
title: jj Security Boundary and Dashboard Run Inspection
